content.text:
Question:
                                How many Instagram followers do I currently have?

                                Question date:
                                2023/06/08 (Thu) 22:24

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                600

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:1cea1afa
            Answer mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-b81eb4cb97cd-claim-4 [ACTIVE] current_metric_value family=current_metric_value: The user had 600 Instagram followers as of 2023-05-25.; scope=User's Instagram follower count in the imported 2023-05-25 session.; context="The user mentioned this milestone while asking for Instagram promotional content ideas."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:89c82239443eaf57de7779c3f5045ae6 quote="I'm now at 600 followers"
- claim hot-path-0d8ce3c47147-claim-9 [ACTIVE] believes family=believes: The user believes their Instagram followers love seeing photos of Luna.; scope=User's understanding of their Instagram audience preferences; context="The user mentioned this as a reason to incorporate Luna into behind-the-scenes Instagram content."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:fe5712f97a449e8389155e997041b7d7 quote="I've noticed that my followers love seeing photos of my cat, Luna"
- claim hot-path-0d8ce3c47147-claim-14 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user plans to post an Instagram Story poll asking followers what they would like to see more of in the user's content.; scope=User's planned Instagram Story poll for content feedback; context="The user selected Instagram's Poll feature as a way to gather follower feedback about content direction."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:fe5712f97a449e8389155e997041b7d7 quote="I'm going to post a poll on my Instagram story asking my followers what they'd like to see more of in my content."
- claim hot-path-0d8ce3c47147-claim-7 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user has a goal to keep the momentum going after reaching 500 Instagram followers.; scope=User's Instagram growth strategy after 500-follower milestone; context="The user described this goal during a discussion of increasing Instagram presence and engagement."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:fe5712f97a449e8389155e997041b7d7 quote="I'm hoping to keep that momentum going"
- claim hot-path-b81eb4cb97cd-claim-12 [ACTIVE] q_and_a_answer_policy family=q_and_a_answer_policy: For the planned Instagram Q&A session, the user plans to communicate with followers and set expectations about answering questions.; scope=User's planned Instagram Q&A session answer strategy.; context="The user said they will set expectations after deciding to answer only a selection of questions."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:89c82239443eaf57de7779c3f5045ae6 quote="I'll make sure to communicate with my followers and set expectations"
- claim hot-path-0d8ce3c47147-claim-12 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user plans to start using Instagram Stories for writing-process content by posting snippets of their manuscript.; scope=User's selected initial Instagram Stories writing-process content; context="The user selected manuscript snippets as the starting implementation of the Instagram Stories writing-process idea."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:fe5712f97a449e8389155e997041b7d7 quote="I think I'll start by posting some snippets of my manuscript"
- claim hot-path-0d8ce3c47147-claim-17 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user plans to create a follow-up Instagram Story showing the poll results with a graphic or screenshot, adding context or insights, thanking participants, and highlighting the winning option.; scope=User's Instagram poll follow-up plan; context="The user chose a follow-up story format to keep followers engaged and informed about content direction after the poll."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:fe5712f97a449e8389155e997041b7d7 quote="I'll create a follow-up story with a graphic or screenshot of the poll results, and add some context or insights about what I learned from the poll."
- claim hot-path-b81eb4cb97cd-claim-13 [ACTIVE] generated_artifact_detail family=generated_artifact_detail: The user's planned Instagram Story reminder says the Q&A session is tomorrow and asks followers to use the Question Sticker to submit questions.; scope=User-provided Instagram Story reminder draft in the imported 2023-05-25 session.; context="The user provided a draft Instagram Story reminder for the planned Q&A session."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:89c82239443eaf57de7779c3f5045ae6 quote="Just a quick reminder that I'll be hosting a Q&A session in my Instagram Story tomorrow!"

            Retrieved notes:
            none

            Retrieved evidence:
            - source external:provided-text:89c82239443eaf57de7779c3f5045ae6 [external_record ref=longmemeval:1cea1afa:session:2 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
...Session date: 2023/05/25 (Thu) 22:45
Transcript:
user: I'm trying to increase my social media presence. Can you help me come up with some ideas for more engaging promotional content on Instagram? By the way, I just checked and I'm now at 600 followers, which is a nice milestone!...
...3. **Q&A Sessions**: Host a weekly or monthly Q&A session where you answer questions from your followers. You can use the "Question Sticker" feature in Instagram Stories or create a post asking followers to submit their questions in the comments.
4. **Exclusive Offers and Discounts**: Create limited-time offers or special discounts for your Instagram followers to incentivize engagement and drive sales. Use Instagram's "Swipe-Up" feature to direct followers to your website or a landing page....
...6. **Collaborate with Influencers**: Partner with influencers in your niche to reach new audiences and create engaging content. Choose influencers who align with your brand values and have a similar target audience....
...12. **Seasonal or Timely Content**: Create content that's relevant to current events, holidays, or trends. This can help you stay topical and attract new followers....
...user: I want to try out the Q&A session idea. Do you think I should use the "Question Sticker" feature in Instagram Stories or create a post asking followers to submit their questions in the comments?...
...user: I think I'll try using the "Question Sticker" feature in Instagram Stories first, since it seems like a quick and easy way to get started. Do you have any tips on how to promote my Q&A session to get more followers to participate?...
..."🤔 Got questions about [topic]? 🤓 I'm hosting a Q&A session in my Instagram Stories tomorrow! 💬 Ask me anything about [topic] and I'll do my best to answer. Use the "Question Sticker" in my story to submit your questions. 📝 Don't forget to follow me for updates and behind-the-scenes peeks! 👉 @[YourHandle] #Ask[YourName] #QandAwith[YourName]"...
...Regarding answering questions, it's completely up to you how many questions you want to answer. Here are some pros and cons of answering all questions versus answering a selection:...
...user: I think I'll answer a selection of questions and address common themes. I'll also provide additional resources if I can't answer every question. I'll make sure to communicate with my followers and set expectations. Thanks for the advice!...
...Most importantly, have fun with it! A Q&A session is a great way to connect with your followers and build a stronger community. Be yourself, be authentic, and show your followers that you care about their questions and concerns....
...user: I'm going to post a quick update on my Instagram Story to remind my followers about the Q&A session tomorrow. Here's what I'll say:

"Hey everyone! 🙋‍♀️ Just a quick reminder that I'll be hosting a Q&A session in my Instagram Story tomorrow! 🤔 Ask me anything about my writing process, freelancing, or anything else you're curious about. 📝 Use the "Question Sticker" to submit your questions, and I'll do my best to answer as many as I can. 🙏 Thanks for participating, and I'm looking forward to chatting with you all tomorrow! 😊 #QandAwith[YourName] #WritingCommunity #FreelanceLife"...
..."Hey everyone! 🙋‍♀️ Just a quick reminder that I'll be hosting a Q&A session in my Instagram Story tomorrow at 10am EST! 🕰️ Ask me anything about my writing process, freelancing, or anything else you're curious about. 📝 Use the "Question Sticker" to submit your questions, and I'll do my best to answer as many as I can. 🙏 Thanks for participating, and I'm looking forward to chatting with you all tomorrow! 😊 #QandAwith[YourName] #WritingCommunity #FreelanceLife"...
- source external:provided-text:fe5712f97a449e8389155e997041b7d7 [external_record ref=longmemeval:1cea1afa:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
...Session date: 2023/05/21 (Sun) 16:22
Transcript:
user: I've been trying to increase my social media presence, especially on Instagram. Can you help me come up with some ideas for more engaging promotional content, like the kind that will resonate with my audience and get more likes and comments? By the way, I just reached 500 followers last week, and I'm hoping to keep that momentum going....
...**2. User-Generated Content (UGC) Campaign**: Encourage your followers to share content related to your brand by:...
...What do you think? Is there a particular type of content that resonates with your audience, or do you have any specific questions about these ideas?
user: I think these are some great ideas, especially the behind-the-scenes content and user-generated content campaign. I've noticed that my followers love seeing photos of my cat, Luna, so maybe I can incorporate her into some BTS content. Do you think I could also use Instagram Stories to share some of my writing process, like snippets of my manuscript or research? And what are some good ways to encourage users to share UGC?...
...Remember to keep your Stories engaging and visually appealing, using text, stickers, and other features to make them fun and interactive. You can also use the "Question" sticker to ask your followers about their own writing processes or favorite books.

**Encouraging User-Generated Content (UGC)**: Here are some ways to encourage your followers to share UGC:...
...2. **Offer incentives**: Provide a reward or incentive for followers who share UGC, such as a discount code, freebie, or feature on your page....
...user: I like the idea of using Instagram Stories to share my writing process, and I think I'll start by posting some snippets of my manuscript. Do you think I should post these snippets as a series, like a "Manuscript Monday" feature, or just post them randomly as I complete new sections?...
...* Use Instagram's built-in features, like the "Question" sticker or polls, to ask your followers about their preferences and adjust your posting schedule accordingly....
...user: I think I'll try the hybrid approach, posting snippets of my manuscript as I complete new sections, but trying to maintain a consistent frequency. I'll also ask my followers about their preferences to see what they think. By the way, do you think I could use Instagram's "Poll" feature to ask my followers what they'd like to see more of in my content, like "More writing tips" or "More behind-the-scenes"?...
...This will not only give you valuable insights into what your followers are interested in but also encourage them to engage with your content more actively. You can even use the results to inform your future content strategy and create more targeted posts that resonate with your audience....
...user: I'm going to post a poll on my Instagram story asking my followers what they'd like to see more of in my content. I'll keep it simple with 3 options: "More writing tips and advice", "More behind-the-scenes content", and "More sneak peeks of my manuscript". I'll also ask them to share their thoughts and opinions in the comments section. Do you think I should share the results of the poll in a follow-up post or story to keep my followers engaged?...
...4. **Instagram Live**: Host an Instagram Live session to discuss the results, answer questions, and provide additional insights into your content strategy....
...user: I think I'll create a follow-up story with a graphic or screenshot of the poll results, and add some context or insights about what I learned from the poll. I'll also thank my followers for participating and providing feedback, and highlight the winning option. This way, I can keep my followers engaged and informed about the direction of my content. And who knows, maybe I'll even get some new ideas for my writing and content creation!...

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [CONCEPT]: User's Instagram Q&A session; Concept named User's Instagram Q&A session.
- entity entity:<1> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.
